_coverage_note: JNTUA (Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University Anantapur, Andhra Pradesh) Engineering colleges: 3 constituent (Ananthapuramu, Pulivendula, Kalikiri) + 84 affiliated, sourced from jntua.ac.in's own official pages. Excludes JNTUA-OTPRI (Oil Technology and Pharmaceutical Research Institute), the 4th constituent unit -- a research institute (est. 1948 as OTRI, renamed 2016), not a B.Tech engineering college, same reasoning as excluding pharmacy-only constituent colleges at JNTUK/JNTUH. Affiliated colleges are from the 5 official per-district pages linked from jntua.ac.in's own 'Affiliated Colleges' index -- Anantapur, Chittoor, Y.S.R, Kurnool, Nellore -- which render as a JavaScript app shell under plain HTTP fetch and required browser rendering to read. Filtered to Engineering only: excludes 17 Pharmacy and 21 Management/MBA/MCA/PG colleges on the same 5 pages. 17 ambiguous names (Group of Institutions / 'Technology and Management' / 'IT and Management' / PG College phrasing that didn't resolve from the name alone) were checked individually against each institution's own website rather than guessed; the resulting classifications and any umbrella-org double-count risk are recorded per-record in `notes`. Two on-page exact-duplicate rows (Annamacharya P.G. College of Computer Studies on the Y.S.R page; Master Institute of Management Science and Technology on the Nellore page) were deduped to one record each -- both are non-Engineering entries excluded from this dataset regardless. VINTAGE: none of the 5 district pages carries an explicit academic-year label. All 5 report an identical document Last-Modified of 2025-11-26 (likely a site-wide rebuild date, not necessarily a per-page data-curation date). No colleges on the current live Y.S.R page carry an asterisk/provisional-code marker, despite earlier sourcing notes describing such a marker and a 2021 date signal on that page -- the live page as fetched (July 2026) shows neither, consistent with the site having been rebuilt/edited since. jntua.ac.in's navigation lists four additional new-district page names (Annamayya, Nandyal, Sri Sathya Sai, Tirupati -- the districts carved out of Anantapur/Kurnool/Y.S.R/Chittoor in Andhra Pradesh's 2022 reorganization) as plain, non-linked menu text; the corresponding URLs (e.g. affiliated-colleges-annamayya) 404, confirming the 5 old-district pages are still the university's actual, complete, live source. CROSS-CHECK: this dataset counts 84 affiliated Engineering colleges. JNTUA's own homepage states 98 affiliated Engineering colleges (also citing 33 Pharmacy and 29 MBA/MCA, versus 17 and 21 counted on these same 5 pages). This does NOT converge, unlike JNTUH's list (137 vs 137). Investigated before shipping: confirmed no pagination hides additional rows on any of the 5 pages; confirmed no further district pages exist beyond the 5 (the 4 new-district names above 404); re-verified all 17 ambiguous entries against each institution's own site. No missing-page or miscount cause was found. Notably, the SAME shortfall pattern holds across all three of JNTUA's own categories on these pages (Engineering 84/98, Pharmacy 17/33, Management/MCA 21/29) -- not just Engineering -- which is evidence against a category-specific miscount on this project's part, and supports the most likely explanation: the homepage's descriptive '98/33/29' figures are a legacy aggregate that hasn't been kept in sync with the live, apparently-pruned district tables (which show a Nov 2025 edit signal). Shipped with the actual count from the live official source rather than padded to match the homepage figure; this gap should be revisited if JNTUA republishes the district pages or an updated aggregate count.

colleges.name: JNTUA College of Engineering, Ananthapuramu
colleges.notes: Flagship constituent college -- originally College of Engineering, Anantapur (started at Guindy, Madras in 1946, shifted to Anantapur in 1948); became a JNTUA constituent college at JNTUA's 2008 formation, per JNTUA's own homepage history.
